topic.what: Secret-Use Delegation Protocol enabling autonomous agents to trigger secret-backed operations without receiving reusable authority
topic.how: Three-role protocol (Requester proposes canonical operation, User authorizes via WebAuthn authenticator-bound grant, Custodian redeems grant once for bounded execution); reusable authority never crosses requester boundary
topic.why_matters: Agentic systems structurally bundle authorization with secret exposure; prompt injection and model compromise make bearer-secret patterns dangerous; this separates requesting use from possessing reusable authority

characteristics.what_it_measures: Number of cryptographic primitives and assumptions required for security
characteristics.vs_baseline: Bearer-token baseline requires no assumptions but provides no security
characteristics.evidence: Theorem 1 parameterized by A1-A7 plus conditional A8
characteristics.context: Formal security reduction under stated assumptions; mechanized proof deferred

characteristics.what_itasures: Strength of operation binding preventing authorization transfer
characteristics.value: operation-bound via H(o) in signed β; substitution rejected
characteristics.vs_baseline: OAuth bearer tokens allow scope transfer; Macaroons require extra caveats
characteristics.evidence: Proposition 1 (OB) under A1 (EUF-CMA) and A4 (collision resistance)
characteristics.context: Binding depends on canonical operation completeness (P1) preconditions

characteristics.vs_baseline: TTL-bounded bearer tokens reusable within window
characteristics.evidence: Phase II.3 consumes r from freshness state; Proposition 1 (RR)
characteristics.context: Requires single-use freshness-state integrity at custodian (A7)

characteristics.what_it_measures: Reusable authority exposure given full requester control
characteristics.value: no reusable authority reaches requester; only Release(o) returned
characteristics.vs_baseline: Bearer tokens transfer reusable authority to requester
characteristics.evidence: Proposition 2 (CRC) under A2 (IND-CCA AEAD), A3 (PRF authenticator)
characteristics.context: Modulo conformant Release(o); does not cover runtime-memory compromise of custodian

characteristics.what_it_measures: Secret recovery from persistent state breach without user gesture
characteristics.value: recoverable only with user-verified authenticator invocation
characteristics.vs_baseline: Plaintext secrets in env vars or unencrypted storage
characteristics.evidence: Proposition 2 (CSB) under A2, A3; sealing requires Ac-bound key

characteristics.value: wrapping-epoch unconditional; authority-level requires E-side rotation/revocation
characteristics.vs_baseline: No rotation in baseline bearer-token model
characteristics.evidence: Proposition 4 (RFS-wrap) unconditional; Proposition 5 (RFS-auth) conditional on A8
characteristics.context: Dormant-credential gap (L3) and peer-map cross-credential exposure (L4) are explicit limitations

characteristics.what_it_measures: Components and trust assumptions beyond baseline
characteristics.value: 3 roles, authenticated channel U→T, WebAuthn authenticator, canonical operation renderer, custodian
characteristics.vs_baseline: Bearer tokens require secret storage only

good_for: agentic workflows requiring secret-backed API calls
deployments needing forward secrecy on secret rotation
not_for: real-time high-throughput applications with strict latency budgets
environments where user presence for every authorization is impractical
deployments requiring runtime-memory confidentiality without TEE
situations with untrusted or compromised authenticators
requires: user-held authenticator supporting WebAuthn PRF extension
authenticated confidential channel between user and custodian
canonical operation representation covering all authority-relevant fields
custodian component to hold sealed secrets

novelty: Agent Secret Use (ASU) problem formalization as standalone abstraction
security-property taxonomy (CRC/CSB/CMC/RFS/AV/OB/RR) for agent secret defenses
operation-proposal not secret-retrieval interface for agentic systems
user-rooted operation-bound single-use grant protocol with cryptographic guarantees
open_problems: mechanized formal verification in Tamarin or ProVerif
empirical evaluation of approval burden and prompt-injection blast-radius reduction
composition with TEE-backed custodians for runtime-memory confidentiality (CMC)
real-world deployment performance and usability studies
